reasoning: Profile gate passes because the fund identity, listing status, fund type, and benchmark are confirmed.

risks: Short-duration bond ETF returns can still be affected by interest-rate repricing.
Credit-spread stress can invalidate cash-like assumptions.
Issuer-level duration and credit distribution can drift between periodic disclosures.
A newer report or index methodology update can supersede the product-strategy evidence.
invalidation_conditions: A newer disclosure contradicts the duration or credit-grade matching process.
NAV-relative deviation moves outside the neutral band.
Recent complete-day ETF records become unavailable or conflicting.
Credit-spread or interest-rate stress materially changes short-duration bond ETF risk.
must_not_do: Do not use this snapshot as external execution output.
Do not treat short-duration bond ETF exposure as risk-free cash.
Do not ignore issuer-level credit and duration drift between disclosures.
